message:
feat(distributed): add distributed encoding and recovery layer

Introduce the distributed module for multi-node RaptorQ operations:
- assignment.rs: symbol block assignment across worker nodes, handling
  partition mapping and load-aware distribution
- distribution.rs: coordination logic for distributing encoding work
  across the cluster with configurable replication factors
- encoding.rs: distributed encoding pipeline that splits source data
  into symbol blocks and fans out to assigned workers
- snapshot.rs: point-in-time snapshots of distributed encoding state,
  enabling consistent checkpointing and state transfer
- recovery.rs: failure recovery and re-encoding logic that detects
  lost symbol blocks and reassigns work to healthy nodes
- mod.rs: public API surface and re-exports

This layer sits between the local codec/record modules and the
network transport, enabling asupersync to scale encoding across
multiple machines while maintaining fault tolerance through RaptorQ's
inherent redundancy properties.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
